learning_outcomes.description: Have an understanding of the methods of analysis, an understanding of a number of the techniques employed in analytical chemistry and the applications of analytical chemistry.
Undertake experiments that require both qualitative and quantitative outcomes and appreciate the requirements for precision and accuracy in laboratory experiments.
Demonstrate core generic skills in analytical chemistry, physical chemistry, inorganic chemistry and organic chemistry.
Perform chemical syntheses that will train the student in particular advanced experimental bench techniques.
Appreciate the many factors that can affect an experiment and to be able to recognise and trouble-shoot experimental difficulties.
Concisely communicate the results of experiments in written laboratory reports and/or oral prac examination.

assessments.task_description:
You are required to purchase and maintain an accurate LABORATORY NOTEBOOK for all experiments. It should be written legibly such that anybody else could follow it and obtain the same results. It should be written in ink/pen, not pencil (nor printed texts, nor chemical structures, except acquired data from equipment). Details of how to keep a Laboratory Notebook is available in a how-to-guide on the Chem Lab Hub. Details of where to buy a Laboratory Notebook is also on the CHEM Lab Hub. A marking sheet will be available to help your understanding of achievements of the requirements.

Lab Notebook marking sheet will be provided in Week 1.

Please note that all Laboratory Notebooks must be not removed from Level 5 of Chemistry Building. If a Laboratory Notebook is not present in the lab when marking takes place, zero marks will be awarded for the section being marked.


Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The Laboratory Notebook cannot be removed from Level 5 of Chemistry Building (68) for assessment marking. If you will need your lab Notebook for other courses, talk to or email the course coordinator (A/Prof Lo, s.lo@uq.edu.au) for approval. Marking criteria are available in the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd>45% Experiment 1: pass/fail; Exp 2-4: 5% each: Exp 5-7: 10% each.  </dd>

assessments.task_description:
Weightings:

Experiment 1: Pass/Fail; Fail = failure of the course.

Experiments 2-4: 15% (i.e. 5% each).

Experiments 5-7: 30% (i.e. 10% each).


The completion of a hard copy sheet for Experiment 1 during week 1 is required to continue in the course. The completion results sheets is required for the Core Skills Experiments (Experiments 2-4). The completion of a guided report is required for the Analytical Experiments. The results sheets and guided report templates can be obtained from Blackboard. It is your responsibility to think about what key information must be recorded in the laboratory book during the lab sessions. The results sheets and guided reports are submitted via Blackboard. These experiments are assessed on the basis of the precision and accuracy of your results as well as based on clarity in presentation of data and calculations. You will be required to include error analysis where applicable. 


Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. 


Absence from a Practical Session

You must attend 100% of the laboratory component of this course.

If you are unable to attend your timetabled practical session due to circumstances beyond your control (e.g. illness), you must submit a practical absence request immediately accompanied by acceptable supporting documentation that covers the date of the missed practical.

A list of acceptable reasons and required supporting documentation can be found at https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/applying-assessment-extension?p=1#1

Requests for practical absences can be submitted via the course Blackboard site.

Your request must be submitted prior to or on the day of the missed practical. It is your responsibility to submit an application and provide correct documentation by the due date and time. Requests without correct supporting documentation may not be approved. If the reason for your absence is not approved, you will be considered as absent from that practical session and the laboratory attendance hurdle may apply.

Extensions will not be granted for alternative assessment (unless exceptional circumstances can be demonstrated).

26/10/2026 - 30/10/2026

The Project Report is due at 5 pm prior to your prac day of Week 13 through Turnitin (on the course Blackboard site) submission.

The Project Oral Examination will take place in your allocated practical session of Week 13. The specific timetable will be available on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.task_description:
The "Project Oral Examination" will cover selected material from the lectures (Weeks 8-12), and concepts and contents related to your project. During the exam you will be able to refer to your project report and/or lab notebook. This part of the assessment is worth a total of 10%. The Project Oral Exam will be held in week 13.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

For Project Report: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. 


For Project Oral Examination: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The Exam will cover selected material from the lectures (Weeks 1-7), and concepts and contents from Experiments 1-7, as well as the Essential chemistry knowledge (details available on Blackboard). The Exam will have computer access but no internet access. Specific material will be made available during the exam. A full list will be provided on Blackboard. The assessment is worth a total of 20% (Exp 1-7: 15% + Essential chemistry knowledge: 5%). The Exam will be held at the end of semester examination period. This is a centrally-scheduled School-based exam and location details will be shared once the end of semester exam timetable is released.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(27 Jul - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture

The scheduled lecture time (Mondays) will be used for the delivery of lectures on analytical chemistry, data analysis, core chemistry skills, and projects as well as in-class discussion. The content of these lectures are relevant to the material in the laboratory course and more generally. You are STRONGLY advised to commit to attending the lecture.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture The scheduled lecture time (Mondays) will be used for the delivery of lectures on analytical chemistry, data analysis, core chemistry skills, and projects as well as in-class discussion. The content of these lectures are relevant to the material in the laboratory course and more generally. You are STRONGLY advised to commit to attending the lecture.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(27 Jul - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Laboratory experiments

Students are required to complete a series of set laboratory experiments, commencing from Week 1. These are designed to cover various topics in analytical chemistry, physical chemistry, inorganic chemistry and organic chemistry. There is also an extended project. For this extended project, there will be a choice of experiments - the choices will be detailed on the CHEM3016 Blackboard page. You choose ONE project. You work alone, NOT in PAIRS. Collusion will be STRONGLY discouraged. There will be a minimum number of "places" available for each experiment and once those places are full that particular experiment is considered closed. An online signing system will be used.

Risk assessment for each prac must be completed prior to each scheduled prac session for approval. If your risk assessment will not be endorsed before 10:15am of your scheduled prac day, you need to reschedule the prac, where reschedule penalty will apply--see reschedule in the Addition Information.

For each scheduled pac day, students are expected to spend 6 hours in the lab to conduct the scheduled lab work, including cleaning and data analysis as well as report writing.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
